title: Route dogfooding pressure into issues and memory

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Route the recent dogfooding feedback into durable issues and Memory pressure.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Create or update narrow GitHub issues for actionable dogfooding findings and update only the relevant Memory routing note; do not implement product fixes in this slice.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Choose issue-vs-memory ownership, avoid duplicate issues, and keep the memory update compact.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A finding is ambiguous product direction, needs prioritization, or would require changing package behavior instead of routing evidence.
canonical_core.next_action: Create or update GitHub issues for the concrete findings, update Memory routing guidance, refresh external intent, and validate memory/planning health.
proof_expectations: gh issue list/search plus created/updated issue URLs
uv run agentic-workspace external-intent refresh-github --target . --state all --storage cache --format json
uv run agentic-workspace memory doctor --target . --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Concrete dogfooding findings are routed to existing or new GitHub issues.
The recurring instruction to route dogfooding pressure is captured in Memory.
External intent is refreshed and planning/memory health is checked.
canonical_core.continuation_owner: created/updated issues and Memory routing note

goal: Create a bounded plan for Route dogfooding pressure into issues and memory.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Route the recent dogfooding feedback into durable issues and Memory pressure.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make routine dogfooding feedback route into durable owners without waiting for a one-off reminder.
intent_continuity.continuation surface: created/updated GitHub issues and Memory routing note

required_continuation.activation trigger: When one of the routed issues is selected for implementation.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Dogfooding feedback from PR #1045 is now routed to issues, Memory, and planning evidence instead of remaining chat-only.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Routine dogfooding reflection should include smoothness gaps, improvement suggestions, AW-help gaps, and unused-feature retrospection.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Issues #1046-#1050 created, #1035 updated, Memory notes updated, external intent refreshed, reconcile run, and memory/planning health checked.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Memory doctor reports an unrelated safe memory lifecycle upgrade is available; not applied in this slice.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Select one of #1046-#1050 or #1035 when ready.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Create or update issues; always route routine dogfooding; use Memory for improvement pressure.

intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Routine dogfooding feedback must include smoothness gaps, possible improvements even when things work, AW-help gaps, and unused-feature retrospection, with concrete signals routed.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Avoid chat-only dogfooding reports and make Memory a central improvement-pressure owner when appropriate.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: If this routine needs enforcement beyond Memory, promote to checks, closeout prompts, or product workflow issue #1050.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The routing slice is complete, but the routed issues remain open for implementation.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Issues #1046-#1050 created, #1035 updated, Memory runbooks updated, external intent refreshed and reconciled.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A dogfooding report again stops without issue/Memory/planning/dismissal routing.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Dogfooding report initially stopped at chat-only reporting.
Proof selection missed schema reference docs for schema changes.
Small CI/doc repair slices have heavy scaffold closeout burden.
CI check inspection is noisy and post-push checks can race.
start can over-emphasize next roadmap lane for narrow follow-up tasks.
signals fixed: Routine dogfooding reflection/routing contract captured in Memory.
signals routed: #1050 for first-class routine dogfooding reflection routing.
#1049 for CI check inspection and rerun timing.
signals dismissed: No weak or duplicate signal dismissed; all concrete signals were routed.
